stem: A factory produces solar panels using two sources. Automated Line East handles 57% of production with a known defect rate of 3.9%. Automated Line West handles 43% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 6.1% and 11.8%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected solar panel is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Automated Line West?

ground_truth.answer: P(Automated Line West | defective) ranges from 0.541 to 0.695 as Automated Line West's defect rate varies from 6.1% to 11.8%. Point estimate at 8.9%: 0.633.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.541, 0.695] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
